items.name: One Sec
items.source_story_title: I made my phone slow on purpose
items.summary: One Sec — friction app that adds a pause before opening distracting apps.
items.why_included: Commenters said the pause worked better than simple blockers at breaking the scroll loop.
items.evidence: One user said it helped them delete Instagram and never look back.

items.name: ScreenZen
items.source_story_title: I made my phone slow on purpose
items.summary: ScreenZen — donation-supported screen-time blocker with mindful app-opening prompts.
items.why_included: Readers preferred its lower-friction pricing and behavior-changing prompts over rigid blockers.
items.evidence: A commenter said it was their preferred blocker and cited the one-time tip model.

items.name: AppBlock
items.source_story_title: I made my phone slow on purpose
items.summary: AppBlock — Android app blocker with timed lockouts and website blocking.
items.why_included: The thread highlighted a stricter wait-and-relock flow for people who bypass normal limits.
items.evidence: A commenter described waiting three minutes without switching apps, then relocking 15 minutes later.

items.name: StepLimit
items.source_story_title: I made my phone slow on purpose
items.summary: StepLimit — iPhone app that unlocks app time only after you walk.
items.why_included: The walking-for-time mechanic turned attention control into an explicit earned reward.
items.evidence: One commenter liked earning minutes on tracked apps by walking.

items.name: DOSBox-X
items.source_story_title: Windows GOG DOS Games on M-Series Macs
items.summary: DOSBox-X — actively maintained DOSBox fork with broader compatibility and extras.
items.why_included: Commenters treated modern DOSBox forks as the real answer for Mac DOS gaming.
items.evidence: A commenter said vanilla DOSBox was least interesting and listed DOSBox-X among the main maintained forks.

items.name: DOSBox Staging
items.source_story_title: Windows GOG DOS Games on M-Series Macs
items.summary: DOSBox Staging — polished DOSBox continuation with current development and filters.
items.why_included: Readers praised its active maintenance and modern polish over the frozen original.
items.evidence: The thread singled it out alongside DOSBox-X and praised its CRT-style display filters.

items.name: DOSBox Pure
items.source_story_title: Windows GOG DOS Games on M-Series Macs
items.summary: DOSBox Pure — RetroArch-friendly DOSBox fork that bundles games into single zips.
items.why_included: The packaging model makes classic DOS games easier to manage and launch.
items.evidence: A commenter liked putting full games into one zip file and using it like a console-style bundle.

items.name: Boxer
items.source_story_title: Windows GOG DOS Games on M-Series Macs
items.summary: Boxer — Mac DOS game launcher remembered for the nicest retro-game UX.
items.why_included: The thread kept returning to Boxer as the most elegant Mac front end for old DOS titles.
items.evidence: Commenters called it wonderful in its day and noted GOG used it for Mac DOS releases.

items.name: Expanse
items.source_story_title: Launch HN: Expanse (YC P26) – Unlock Wasted GPU Capacity
items.summary: Expanse — cluster prediction layer that reduces wasted GPU and HPC capacity.
items.why_included: It is a concrete launch solving an expensive infrastructure problem with measurable waste.
items.evidence: The launch thread said it predicts what a job actually needs before submission to cut waste.

items.name: PolyCSS
items.source_story_title: Show HN: A CSS 3D Engine (no WebGL)
items.summary: PolyCSS — CSS-based 3D engine that renders low-poly scenes in the DOM.
items.why_included: Commenters liked the unusual tradeoff for small scenes where DOM integration matters.
items.evidence: The thread benchmarked it against Three.js and discussed the low-poly DOM approach.

items.name: Gitea
items.source_story_title: GitHub and the crime against software
items.summary: Gitea — self-hosted Git service suggested as a lighter GitHub alternative.
items.why_included: The GitHub critique thread repeatedly pushed self-hosting and modular tool stacks.
items.evidence: Commenters suggested self-hosted forges and migration-friendly alternatives instead of GitHub lock-in.

items.name: llama.cpp
items.source_story_title: A 10 year old Xeon is all you need
items.summary: llama.cpp — C/C++ LLM inference project commenters praised over Ollama.
items.why_included: It surfaced as a concrete local-AI tool with a better performance reputation.
items.evidence: A commenter said llama.cpp is much faster and better than Ollama, though less user-friendly.
